heading: Omitted
label: 61a–1, 61a–2 Omitted
notes: CodificationSection 61a–1, acts , ; , , , provided for rate of compensation of Chief Clerk of Senate which office was superseded by Assistant Secretary of Senate.June 27, 1956, ch. 453, § 10170 Stat. 356Pub. L. 92–51, § 10185 Stat. 125July 9, 1971Section 61a–2, , , ; , , , provided for rate of compensation for Postmaster and Assistant Postmaster of Senate. See which abolished all statutory positions in Office of Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of Senate, with specified exceptions, effective , and authorized Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of Senate to appoint and fix compensation of such employees as appropriate.Pub.
